summary: Large gmstaffroll decompilation PR that replaced two remaining staff-roll stubs with C implementations, improved nearby data and text/GObj typings, and propagated a baselib GObj GX-link global rename. The decomp.dev bot reported major matching progress for main/melee/gm/gmstaffroll: fn_801AB200 from 0.00% to 93.13%, fn_801AAB74 from 0.00% to 91.86%, .sdata2 from 4.08% to 94.00%, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter from 96.48% to 99.35%. No human review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented fn_801AAB74 in src/melee/gm/gmstaffroll.c, replacing a decomp placeholder with staff-roll JObj traversal, text-window creation/destruction, language-dependent page selection, matrix setup, and depth sorting of visible staff entries.

Implemented fn_801AB200 in src/melee/gm/gmstaffroll.c, replacing a decomp placeholder with cursor input handling, hover/selection logic, text color changes, particle/JObj interactions, projection math, and staff-entry state updates.
Added typed staff-roll data structures: staffInfo_t, staffInfoSortBuf_t, SortBufEntry, and StaffEntryData, including STATIC_ASSERTs for known sizes 0x948 and 0x2E68.

Improved staff-roll object typing by using HSD_Text* for staffInfo windows, gm_804D680C, and gm_80480D58 instead of anonymous padded structs, allowing direct access to HSD_Text fields such as entity, active_color, font_size, default_alignment, and default_kerning.
Added required includes for the newly decompiled code, including particle, controller, debug, sislib, math_ppc, trigf, psappsrt, and psstructs headers.
Renamed sbss symbols gm_804D67F8 and gm_804D67FC to staffInfo and staffInfoSortBuf in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.

Renamed the baselib global HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead in symbols, declarations, definitions, initialization, GX-link insertion/removal helpers, render traversal code, and existing users in grpushon, grzebes, lbshadow, and gmstaffroll.
Introduced const int Gm_GObj_GXLink_PlyCursor = 9 and used it in a staff-roll HSD_ASSERT against HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
Converted some explicit __assert calls in gmstaffroll rendering code to HSD_ASSERT with line numbers, preserving assertion semantics in a style used elsewhere in the project.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt for staff-roll and GObj names, including staffInfo, staffInfoSortBuf, HSD_GObjGXLinkHead, and a local .data symbol at 0x803DBFD8 renamed to z in the symbol file while the source still uses gm_803DBFD8 for the StaffEntryData array.
smart_moves: Used concrete HSD_Text* and HSD_JObj* fields instead of anonymous padding where the diff showed enough evidence, which made the decompiled staff-roll code more readable and reduced brittle offset-based access.
Created SortBufEntry to model the temporary render/depth-sort buffer as index, jobj, matrix, and trailing s32 fields, matching how fn_801AAB74 computes matrices and bubble-sorts by mtx[2][3].
Kept size assertions on large partially understood staff-roll buffers, preserving layout confidence while still allowing partial struct recovery.
Propagated HSD_GObjGXLinkHead rename across baselib and all immediate users in the same PR, preventing mixed old/new names for the same global.

Used language helpers lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP, lbLang_IsSettingJP, and lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS directly in the staff-roll logic, documenting that page selection and hitbox sizing are language-dependent.
Modeled text creation through HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC and page assignment through HSD_SisLib_803A6368, which exposes the staff-roll as SIS text-window logic rather than raw GObj manipulation.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function manipulates SIS text windows through a fake struct containing an HSD_GObj pointer at offset 0x54, check whether the object is actually HSD_Text and whether the field should be named entity.
For render ordering code, a small typed sort-buffer struct with cached matrices can clarify the original algorithm and help match loops that sort visible objects by camera-space depth.
Global symbol renames in baselib should be applied consistently through symbols.txt, headers, definitions, allocation/init code, and all cross-module users in the same change.
Partial struct recovery can safely coexist with padding and STATIC_ASSERTs; this PR names the known surface area while keeping unknown large buffers constrained by exact size.
Staff-roll logic is tightly coupled to language/page data; future work should preserve separate JP/EN page computations and special-case checks instead of simplifying them away.
Replacing __assert blocks with HSD_ASSERT can be a matching/readability tactic when line numbers and expressions are known and the macro is accepted by the codebase.
The decomp.dev report can be used as objective evidence of partial-match quality even when functions are not fully matched: here the major functions reached roughly 92-93% rather than 100%.
naming_conventions: Recovered staff-roll globals use camelCase descriptive names: staffInfo and staffInfoSortBuf.
Recovered local types use PascalCase or lower-camel-with-_t style depending on context: SortBufEntry, StaffEntryData, staffInfo_t, staffInfoSortBuf_t.
Baselib GObj global naming follows subsystem-prefixed descriptive form: HSD_GObjGXLinkHead instead of address-based HSD_GObj_804D7824.
GX-link constants use a game/system prefix with descriptive role: Gm_GObj_GXLink_PlyCursor.
Unknown fields still use offset-style names such as x8, x0, x11, pad_01, pad_0A, and pad_13 when semantics are not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in newly implemented functions, with 0x20 in fn_801AAB74 and 0x60 in fn_801AB200, indicating stack-shaping was needed for matching.
Preserved exact float constants such as 0.209f, 0.13679999f, 0.1f, 0.00038461538f, 0.29411763f, 1.1917536, and 0.008726646471282037 in the cursor/text math.
Used explicit branchy deadzone handling for stick input instead of abstract helpers, likely to preserve original control flow.
Used direct pointer chasing through particle/app structures in fn_801AB200 where better types were not yet evident, avoiding speculative struct invention.
Used HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, HSD_CObjSetupViewingMtx, PSMTXConcat, PSMTXCopy, and PSMTXMultVec directly to mirror original camera-space matrix/projection work.
Kept simple nested-loop bubble sort for staffInfoSortBuf depth ordering rather than replacing with library sorting, preserving original algorithm shape.
The bot-reported matching improvements were partial, not complete: fn_801AB200 reached 93.13% and fn_801AAB74 reached 91.86%.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in this PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported four improvements in unmatched items for main/melee/gm/gmstaffroll: fn_801AB200, fn_801AAB74, .sdata2, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching fn_801AB200 or fn_801AAB74 after the 93.13% and 91.86% progress reported here.
Search for subsequent cleanup of StaffEntryData, especially the pointer arithmetic around gm_803DBFD8/tree_base and particle data in fn_801AB200.
Search for other users of HSD_GObjGXLinkHead to verify the rename fully replaced HSD_GObj_804D7824 across the repository.
Investigate why config/GALE01/symbols.txt renamed the local .data symbol at 0x803DBFD8 to z while gmstaffroll.c still uses gm_803DBFD8 for StaffEntryData.
Look for later type improvements to HSD_Text, HSD_psAppSRT, HSD_Generator, and baselib particle structs that could remove remaining pointer chasing in gmstaffroll.
